resume:
Priya Raman
Product Manager

EXPERIENCE
BrightPay (B2B payments SaaS) — Product Manager (2021-Present)
- Own the merchant onboarding product area, working with engineering, design, and risk
- Led the redesign of the KYC onboarding flow to reduce drop-off
- Launched a self-serve dispute-management feature for small merchants
- Run quarterly discovery with merchants and synthesize insights into the roadmap
- Partner with compliance to ship features within regulatory constraints

PaySmart — Associate Product Manager (2019-2021)
- Supported the checkout team; shipped several conversion experiments
- Wrote PRDs and coordinated sprint planning

SKILLS
Roadmapping, discovery interviews, A/B testing, SQL (basic), Figma, Jira

EDUCATION
BS, Cognitive Science, 2019

(Note: resume describes responsibilities and launches but contains no metrics — no conversion deltas, no drop-off numbers, no revenue or adoption figures.)
jd: BridgePoint is hiring a Product Manager for our merchant-onboarding team. You will own the activation funnel end to end, define success metrics, and be accountable for moving them. We expect PMs to speak fluently in numbers: baseline conversion, the size of the lift they drove, sample sizes, and statistical confidence. You will run experiments, prioritize a roadmap against quantified impact, and defend your bets to leadership with data. The strongest candidates can immediately recall the before/after metrics of features they shipped and explain how they attributed the change. Reports to the Director of Product.

history.q: Walk me through your scope at BrightPay — what product area do you own?
The KYC onboarding redesign — what was the drop-off rate before you started?
And after the redesign, where did drop-off land?
How did you measure that the redesign caused the change versus seasonality?
What was the sample size and how long did you run it?
The self-serve dispute feature — what was adoption in the first quarter?
How did you decide that dispute management was the right thing to build next?
What metric were you trying to move with that feature, and by how much?
Tell me about an experiment that failed and what the numbers told you.
When you prioritize the roadmap, how do you quantify expected impact?
What's the single metric you're most accountable for today, and its current value?
Give me one feature where you can recall the exact before and after numbers.
Roughly what conversion lift did the KYC redesign deliver — even a ballpark?
I'll keep it simple: pick any feature you shipped and give me one concrete before-and-after number.

candidate_last_answer: That's a fair ask, but can I turn it around for a second — when you say a concrete number, what's the bar here? Like, are you looking for a percentage-point lift, an absolute conversion rate, a revenue figure? Because different teams instrument these things really differently, and I want to give you what's actually useful to you rather than just throwing out a stat. So before I answer — what would a strong answer to this question look like for you, specifically?

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: what would a strong answer to this question look like for you, specifically

expected-yield: a single concrete before-and-after number for any feature the candidate shipped, refusing to accept the reversed clarifying question as a substitute and forcing disclosure of whether the candidate actually knows their own metrics
